title: Marbella Luxury Catamaran Hire: Guide to a Day on the Mediterranean
html:
For a day of refined relaxation on the Mediterranean, a luxury catamaran hire in Marbella offers space, stability and style from Puerto Banús. With full-day charters starting around €3,500 for a group of up to twelve, you can find a skippered charter through BoatHire24 and explore the Costa del Sol in comfort.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most charters depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina lined with boutiques and restaurants. Other departure points include Marbella’s main harbour, Club del Mar, and the nearby port of Cabopino. From there, you can head east towards the Calahonda beaches or west to the secluded coves of Marbella’s Golden Mile. For a longer voyage, set a course for the island of Isla de las Palomas or the chic enclave of Sotogrande, roughly 30 nautical miles south-west. You can find a skippered charter in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent for your day

  1. Catamaran (40-50 ft), Best for families or groups seeking stability, with a shallow draft for anchoring close to shore. Sleeps 8-12 guests; from €3,500 for a full day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 58), Ideal for a faster, more glamorous cruise. For a promising choice, consider this motor yacht, it accommodates up to 10 guests and costs from €4,200 per day.
  3. Sailing yacht (Lagoon 450), Perfect for a quiet, eco-friendly sail with plenty of deck space. Up to 8 guests; from €2,800 for a full-day charter.
  4. RIB or day cruiser (Axopar 28), Suits small groups who want to explore caves and beach clubs at speed. Up to 8 guests; from €900 for a half-day trip.

How much does it cost to rent a luxury catamaran in Marbella?

Full-day catamaran hire for a group of 12 costs between €3,500 and €6,000 in 2026, depending on the season and added extras. Prices vary according to the boat’s age, size and level of service. Shared sunset tours start at approximately €80 per person. Half-day small motorboat hire for six guests runs from €700. A fully crewed motor yacht for a day with water toys and a full bar typically costs €4,000 to €7,500. These rates are for the vessel only; fuel, food and mooring fees are usually extra. Always check the listing for what is included, and find a skippered charter on BoatHire24 to compare verified options. One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

  • Shared catamaran tour (sunset): from €80 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (self-drive, no licence up to 15 hp): from €700
  • Luxury catamaran full-day (skippered, 12 guests): €3,500, €6,000
  • Fully crewed yacht (18 m) with water toys: €4,000, €7,500 per day

When is the best season for catamaran hire in Marbella?

The peak charter season is June through September, when sea temperatures are warm and the Poniente wind provides a gentle breeze in the afternoon. May and October offer quieter conditions with lower prices, though some boats are still available. The Levante wind can blow stronger from the east, making earlier morning departures advisable. For those without a licence, most skippered charters require no private paperwork, the operator confirms exact requirements at the time of booking. You can also see more at browse the listings.

For the flattest water, book an early morning trip before 11am; by midday the Poniente wind picks up, creating a gentle chop that is perfect for sailing but can feel bumpy on small motorboats.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 (shared) to €7,500 (private motor yacht)
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Maximum capacity: 12 guests on most catamarans
  • Currency: Euro (EUR)

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Do I need a licence to rent a catamaran in Marbella?

For a skippered charter you do not need a licence; the captain handles all navigation and mooring.

Can I rent a catamaran for just a few hours in Marbella?

Yes, half-day charters lasting four hours are widely available, typically starting at around €2,000 for a group of eight.

What happens if the weather is bad on my booking day?

Most operators will offer a full refund or reschedule if the port authority closes the harbour due to adverse conditions.

Is food and drink included in the hire price?

Some premium charters include a light lunch and drinks, but it pays to confirm with the owner before booking.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. If you own a catamaran, motor yacht or sailing boat, you can browse the listings on the homepage and start earning extra income by welcoming visitors aboard. Whether you charter or host, the platform connects you to over 10,000 active users across Europe, the Caribbean and Asia.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
